campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met on September 2, 2025 to review the city's finances for July, including how the city's money was invested and spent that month. The council also discussed plans to improve transportation options around Campbell, including ways to help people get around by walking, biking, and using transit.
keyTopics: July 2025 city finances and investment accounts
Monthly spending and cash flow report
Campbell transportation improvement plan focusing on multiple travel options

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 where residents could voice their opinions on a proposed new rule or regulation. After hearing public comments, the council decided to move forward by introducing the ordinance and skipping the usual first reading process, with plans to officially adopt it at the next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held for community input
New ordinance introduced and fast-tracked for approval
Adoption scheduled for next regular council meeting
First reading waived to expedite the process

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, primarily to approve meeting minutes from various March sessions and to authorize the Town Manager to move forward with a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The agenda focused on administrative housekeeping and approving a construction project, though the specific details of the construction work are not fully visible in the provided agenda.
keyTopics: Approval of meeting minutes from multiple March 2026 sessions
Construction contract authorization with QLM, Inc.
Town Manager given authority to execute the construction agreement
Review of closed session and special joint study session meetings

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 7, 2026
san-jose.summary: San José City Council met to approve previous meeting records and discussed three main projects: funding for the Mexican Heritage Plaza's School of Arts and Culture, an update on the Bascom Avenue traffic signal project with a consultant, and selecting a contractor for street pavement repairs across the city.
keyTopics: Grant funding for Mexican Heritage Plaza arts and culture programs
Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project progress
Street pavement removal and replacement contract awards
Approval of meeting minutes and committee records

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 7, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met on April 7, 2026, and allowed residents to participate both in person and online. The meeting included instructions for how residents could join the video call or dial in by phone to speak during public comment periods.
keyTopics: Virtual meeting access via Zoom or phone dial-in
Instructions for public comment participation
Hybrid meeting format (in-person and online options)

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 7, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a regular meeting on April 7, 2026, where they recognized Arab-American Heritage Month and Fair Housing Month. The meeting began with a flag salute and included these observances as part of their special order of business.
keyTopics: Arab-American Heritage Month recognition
Fair Housing Month observance
Regular council meeting proceedings

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 7, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to discuss updates on the Active Transportation Plan, which focuses on making it easier for residents to walk and bike around town. The council also recognized April as both Donate Life Month and Fair Housing Month with proclamations. The meeting included time for residents to speak about any issues they wanted to bring before the council.
keyTopics: Active Transportation Plan Phase 2 progress and updates
Changes to the plan's scoring system for evaluating projects
Donate Life Month recognition
Fair Housing Month recognition
Public comment period for residents

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 7, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026, using a hybrid format that allowed both in-person attendance and remote participation. Residents could join online via Zoom or phone to watch the proceedings and submit written comments about agenda items.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format (in-person and remote options available)
Zoom participation details and phone dial-in access
Process for submitting written public comments before meetings
Online agenda and comment submission through legistar platform

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to review several administrative and planning matters, including updates to the city's long-term housing and community development plan for 2025-2030, new work requests from individual council members, and a proposed change to how the city fills council vacancies. The council also approved meeting minutes and reviewed the calendar for upcoming meetings.
keyTopics: Updates to the 2025-2030 Consolidated Plan (housing and federal funding priorities)
Changes to how council vacancies are filled
New work requests from individual council members
Approval of previous meeting minutes and upcoming meeting schedule

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 31, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to approve the city's natural gas utility budget and rates for the next fiscal year. The council considered updating how much residents and businesses pay for gas service, with different rate changes potentially affecting residential customers, apartment buildings, and large commercial users differently.
keyTopics: Natural gas utility budget for fiscal year 2027
Rate changes for residential gas customers (G-1 schedule)
Rate changes for apartment buildings and commercial gas users (G-2 schedule)
Rate changes for large commercial gas customers (G-3 schedule)
Transfer of utility reserves and funds to the general city budget
